learn.title: Solar Inverters: String vs Micro vs Hybrid
learn.intro: Solar inverters convert DC to AC power, enabling grid integration. Understanding their types helps optimize solar investments, reduce costs, and support decentralized energy systems, which are critical for India's renewable energy goals.
learn.c1_text: String inverters connect multiple solar panels in a 'string' to a single inverter. Cost-effective but prone to power loss if one panel is shaded. Ideal for rooftops with uniform sunlight exposure.
learn.c2_text: Micro inverters attach to each panel, optimizing individual output. Reduces 15-20% energy loss from shading or panel mismatch. Enables real-time monitoring and safer installations.
learn.c3_text: Hybrid inverters manage solar, batteries, and grid power simultaneously. Enables energy storage for evening use or grid sell-back. Critical for India’s 500 GW renewable target by 2030.
learn.stat2: String inverters dominate 70% of Indian solar farms.
learn.stat3: Micro inverters boost panel output by up to 25% in shaded areas.
learn.stat4: Hybrid systems reduce grid reliance by 30-40% in off-grid setups.

learn.title: Solid-State Batteries: Safety & Energy Density
learn.intro: Solid-state batteries replace liquid electrolytes with solid materials, offering higher energy density and safety. Key for EVs and grid storage, aligning with India’s push for electric mobility and renewable integration.
learn.c1_text: Solid-state batteries use solid electrolytes, reducing fire risks. Liquid batteries risk leaks and thermal runaway. Solid-state tech enables 2x energy density, critical for EVs.
learn.c2_text: Solid-state batteries pack 400-500 Wh/kg vs. 250 Wh/kg in lithium-ion. Enables longer EV ranges and compact grid storage. India’s EV market could save 100+ million tons of CO2 by 2030.
learn.c3_text: India aims for 30% EV penetration by 2030. Solid-state batteries could cut charging time to 10 minutes and extend battery life to 20+ years. Policy incentives like PLI scheme drive local manufacturing.
learn.stat2: Solid-state batteries reduce thermal runaway risk by 95%.
learn.stat3: Projected 20% cost drop by 2030 for solid-state tech.
learn.stat4: India’s PLI scheme allocates ₹18,100 crore for battery manufacturing.

learn.title: Smart Grids & Advanced Metering
learn.intro: Smart grids use digital tech to monitor and manage electricity flow. Advanced metering enables real-time data, reducing losses and integrating renewables. Vital for India’s 24/7 power vision.
learn.c1_text: Smart grids use sensors and AI to balance supply-demand. Reduces 15-20% transmission losses. Enables bidirectional power flow for solar/wind integration.
learn.c2_text: AMI includes smart meters that provide 15-minute consumption data. Helps utilities detect theft and optimize billing. In Delhi, AMI reduced losses by 8% in pilot zones.
learn.c3_text: India’s Smart Grid Mission targets 50% renewable integration by 2030. 1.2 million smart meters installed in Gujarat and Rajasthan. Policy mandates 100% AMI rollout by 2025.
learn.stat2: Smart grids cut outage duration by 40% in pilot states.
learn.stat3: AMI adoption saves 12% on average electricity bills.
learn.stat4: India’s Smart Grid Mission budget: ₹12,500 crore (2021-2026)

learn.title: Second-Life EV Batteries for Grid Storage
learn.intro: Used EV batteries retain 70-80% capacity and can store solar/wind energy. Reduces waste and costs for grid storage, supporting India’s renewable integration.
learn.c1_text: EV batteries with >70% capacity can store 5-10 kWh per module. Extends lifespan by 5-10 years. Reduces raw material demand by 30% for grid storage.
learn.c2_text: Second-life batteries cost 30-50% less than new ones. Used in India’s first 10 MW solar-plus-storage project in Tamil Nadu. Efficiency matches new batteries at 95%.
learn.c3_text: India’s PLI scheme incentivizes battery reuse. NTPC’s 2023 project in Uttar Pradesh uses 500+ second-life batteries. Policy aims to recycle 90% of EV batteries by 2030.
learn.stat2: Second-life batteries reduce grid storage costs by ₹20,000/kWh.
learn.stat3: Tamil Nadu’s project saves 2,000 tons of CO2 annually.
learn.stat4: India’s battery recycling target: 500 GWh by 2030

learn.title: Solar Panels: Mono vs Poly vs Thin-Film
learn.intro: Solar panel types vary in efficiency, cost, and durability. Choosing the right type impacts solar ROI and suitability for India’s diverse climates.
learn.c1_text: Monocrystalline panels (18-22% efficiency) use single-crystal silicon. Outperforms in low-light and high heat. Ideal for rooftops with limited space.
learn.c2_text: Polycrystalline panels (15-17% efficiency) use silicon fragments. Cheaper but less efficient. Suitable for large-scale farms with ample space.
learn.c3_text: Thin-film panels (10-13% efficiency) use amorphous silicon. Flexible and heat-tolerant. Deployed in Gujarat’s Bhadla Solar Park (58 GW capacity).
learn.stat2: Monocrystalline panels generate 20-25% more power per sqm.
learn.stat3: Thin-film costs 20% less than crystalline panels.
learn.stat4: Bhadla Solar Park powers 1.5 million homes annually.

learn.title: Battery Management Systems (BMS)
learn.intro: BMS monitors battery health, temperature, and charging. Prevents overcharging/overheating, extending lifespan. Critical for India’s EV and renewable storage growth.
learn.c1_text: BMS balances cell voltages, prevents over-discharge, and manages temperature. Reduces battery failure risk by 60%. Essential for lithium-ion packs in EVs and solar storage.
learn.c2_text: BMS uses cooling systems to maintain 20-40°C operating range. Overheating cuts battery life by 50%. India’s hot climate demands advanced thermal management.
learn.c3_text: BIS (Bureau of Indian Standards) mandates BMS for all EVs. Tata Motors’ Nexon EV uses BMS to extend battery life to 10 years. Policy targets 100% BMS compliance by 2025.
learn.stat2: BMS improves battery cycle life by 30-40%.
learn.stat3: Thermal runaway risk drops by 80% with BMS.
learn.stat4: India’s BMS market to reach $2.1B by 2030
